groups.label: Blink Token Loops

groups.description: The Necrobloom wants dredge, deserts, and land recursion that turn the graveyard into a constant stream of landfall and bodies.

groups.label: Zombie Reanimation Engine
groups.description: The Scarab God wants graveyard setup, recursive Zombies, and cost reducers that make every activation punishing.

groups.label: Demonstrate Copy Core
groups.description: The Twelfth Doctor wants cast-from-exile effects and efficient copy targets so demonstrate turns routine spells into big tempo swings.

groups.label: Dragon Ramp Core
groups.description: The Ur-Dragon wants cost reduction, haste pressure, and card-draw payoffs that reward every dragon cast.

groups.label: Rad Counter Core
groups.description: The Wise Mothman wants mill, proliferate, and counter enhancers that turn radiation and +1/+1 counters into the same snowball.

groups.label: Discard Pressure
groups.description: Tinybones wants repeatable discard, cheap hand disruption, and payoffs that convert empty hands into a draw engine and finisher.

groups.label: Artifact Vote Engine
groups.description: Tivit wants artifact payoffs and extra-turn or Time Sieve lines that cash in Clues and Treasures.

groups.label: Saga Value Chain
groups.description: Tom Bombadil wants dense Sagas and enchantment support that keep lore counters moving while each Saga replaces itself.

groups.label: Wide Combat Draw
groups.description: Toski wants evasive creatures and token makers so combat damage turns into a steady stream of cards without overcommitting to sweepers.

groups.label: Wolf Pressure
groups.description: Tovolar wants cheap Wolves and Werewolves, combat support, and ways to keep day/night favorable.

groups.label: Chip-Damage Draw Engines
groups.description: Tymna wants cheap evasive attackers and efficient protection so each combat step safely converts life into multiple extra cards.

groups.label: Eldrazi Copy Core
groups.description: Ulalek wants huge Eldrazi, cast triggers, and mana engines that make copying colorless spells actually realistic.

groups.label: Dragon Hit Density
groups.description: Ureni wants a high Dragon count and topdeck manipulation so each attack converts into another meaningful Dragon entering for free.

groups.label: Myr Swarm Engine
groups.description: Urtet wants dense Myr mana creatures and untap or anthem support that make each new Myr both ramp and pressure.

groups.label: Construct Swarm
groups.description: Urza wants cheap artifact creatures, artifact token makers, and anthem effects that turn each end-step Construct into a real clock.

groups.label: Artifact Velocity
groups.description: Urza wants cheap artifacts and mana-positive loops that convert artifact count into storm turns.

groups.label: Life-Loss Value Core
groups.description: Valgavoth wants constant life-loss sources and punishment pieces that keep cards flowing while the table gets squeezed.

groups.label: Magecraft Storm
groups.description: Veyran wants cheap spells and magecraft payoffs that double into explosive card draw and mana turns.

groups.label: First-Spell Damage Bursts
groups.description: Vial Smasher wants high-mana-value spells and instant-speed interaction so each turn cycle throws meaningful random damage around the table.

groups.label: Elf-Wolf Snowball
groups.description: Voja wants Elf density, combat payoffs, and anthem effects that make every attack step convert into cards and counters.

groups.label: Zombie Attrition
groups.description: Wilhelt wants recursive Zombies, sacrifice outlets, and death payoffs that keep the board stocked.

groups.label: Non-Human Pressure
groups.description: Winota wants cheap non-Humans that attack safely and Humans that end the game on hit.

groups.label: Political Slug Pressure
groups.description: Xantcha wants table-pressure enchantments and mana support so the activated ability becomes a shared draw engine that also drains the chosen opponent down.

groups.label: One-Shot Beaters
groups.description: Xenagos wants trampling haymakers and extra-combat pressure so every combat step threatens to remove a player outright.

groups.label: Wheel Snake Engine
groups.description: Xyris wants wheel effects and token payoffs that turn each draw burst into a lethal pile of Snakes.

groups.label: Double ETB Core
groups.description: Yarok wants ETB permanents and landfall engines that become overwhelming when every trigger happens twice.

groups.label: Odd-CMC Topdeck
groups.description: Yennett wants topdeck control and impactful odd mana value spells so each attack either cheats a haymaker or draws the next one.

groups.label: Food Sacrifice Core
groups.description: Ygra wants Food production and sacrifice payoffs that turn every edible permanent into removal, draw, or pressure.

groups.label: Verse Chain Toolbox
groups.description: Yisan wants a precise creature curve so each verse counter advances into the next tutor target without dead numbers in the chain.

groups.label: Desert Recursion Core
groups.description: Yuma wants deserts, discard or self-mill, and land recursion engines that keep the graveyard fueling your board.

groups.label: Ninja Tempo
groups.description: Yuriko wants unblockable enablers and topdeck manipulation that convert combat triggers into damage.

groups.label: Targeted Cantrip Storm
groups.description: Zada wants a wide board of tokens and cheap single-target cantrips so each spell copies across the team for explosive draws and damage.

groups.label: Donate Control Package
groups.description: Zedruu wants harmless-to-you permanents to donate and prison pieces that become asymmetrical once someone else owns them.

groups.label: Big-Mana Cascade
groups.description: Zhulodok wants explosive mana rocks and tutors for giant colorless spells that cascade into more haymakers.

groups.label: Lands and Card Flow
groups.description: Zimone and Dina want extra land drops, sacrifice fodder, and landfall payoffs that turn every draw step into pressure.

groups.label: Three-Mana Enchantment Toolbox
groups.description: Zur wants silver-bullet enchantments and compact engines so each attack finds the exact lock piece, aura, or card-advantage engine needed.
